signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long is small and explicitly protected on exchange with live reduce-only stop near 2352 and TP near 2458, so this is a bounded-risk management hold rather than an urgent cleanup exit. VETO regime still argues against rotating into anything new while ETH remains manageable.

BTC cooled from the prior squeeze day and is trading under a live VETO headline set. Structure is still constructive enough not to force a bearish flip, but not clean enough to add or rotate from the existing ETH management position.
SOL is soft but still broadly tied to the same post-squeeze tape as BTC and ETH. Under position-management priority plus VETO, it is not cleaner than simply holding the small protected ETH position.
TST is still elevated after a sharp squeeze-style move and remains too extended for fresh long chasing. VETO plus existing ETH exposure keeps it watch-only.
LDO still has constructive recovery structure, but it is already into rebound territory and does not clearly outrank the live ETH hold. VETO keeps it on the watchlist rather than actionable.
MERL is rebounding but the move is not differentiated enough to justify rotating away from the current ETH position. Event brake and execution quality both argue for patience.
FARTCOIN is green and active, but this is exactly the kind of noisy, momentum-heavy name that becomes expensive to manage under VETO. Not cleaner than the bounded-risk ETH hold.
GRIFFAIN is weak on the day, but after the drop it is already messy rather than a clean fresh short. Position management priority says avoid forcing a second idea while ETH is already on the book.
KNEIRO lacks usable live price and candle data in this request, so there is not enough reliable execution context to support any action. Clear no-trade hold.
ALGO recovery remains constructive but not exceptional. Under VETO it is still more of a watchlist name than something worth rotating into from the existing protected ETH position.
ORDI has started cooling after its rebound and now sits in a mixed zone rather than a clean continuation or clean failure. That leaves it as hold-only under the live event brake.
CC still shows some squeeze support, but positive funding and a VETO board reduce the quality of any fresh long. Better to keep it watch-only.

HEMI is the cleanest secondary bearish lean on the sheet because price and OI still fit fresh-shorts-pressing behavior, but this is position management first and not a strong enough edge to override the existing ETH hold.
WCT still has squeeze fuel underneath, but it is too event-sensitive and too extended to trust as a fresh long under VETO. Remains watch-only.
STABLE has the strongest raw negative-funding squeeze profile on the board, but price/OI still looks unstable and headline-sensitive. Under VETO that keeps it out of the action set.
GRASS reads more like short-covering than fresh bullish leadership. That is not strong enough to justify a rotation away from the live ETH management hold.
SUPER is one of the better raw squeeze names, but it is already notably extended and sits inside a VETO regime. Good watchlist candidate, not a position-management override.
MON is still trend-supported, but positive funding and recent expansion make it a lower-quality fresh entry here. Holding existing ETH risk is cleaner than adding MON exposure.
BOME has strong rebound stats but also elevated cascade and extension risk after the run. VETO plus existing ETH exposure keeps it at hold.
PNUT has squeeze support from negative funding, but the setup is still incomplete and not better than simply managing the live ETH position. Watch-only under the active event brake.
